title: Why would the FAR send a real being instead of just the LCL
display_title: Why would the FAR send a real being instead of just the LCL
wikitext:
[]: Why would the FAR send a real being instead of just the LCL?             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Wed Sep 01, 2010 2:55 pm We all know that the conflict in NGE is caused by the two seeds of life landing on the same planet. We also know that humanity evolved naturally from the LCL dispersed by Lilith (although we don't know, or at least I don't, how the angels were created from Adam). My question is why not just send the liquid, LCL, out to planets instead of taking the risk of any one being fusing with both beings, like Rei did in EoE? It seems very, well, stupid to take that sort of risk when all you really needed was the LCL. The only reason I can think of is that Lilith was some sort of animal on the FAR's homeworld that naturally made LCL, and so was Adam. But if that's the case, why would the fusing of the two make a godlike creature, if they were simply creatures. Is there any reason why the two Seeds were basically demi-gods, and if so, why would the FAR risk sending them away?

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Wed Sep 01, 2010 3:13 pm I'm thinking that the seeds also serve a spiritual, rather than just a biological, purpose. There is talk in the series about the doors of guf being associated with the seeds (even though the series never explains what the "doors of guf" are).

[]:             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Wed Sep 01, 2010 3:48 pm It's fairly hazy, but the Seeds of Life are implied to be the source of all souls (Lilith in particular, as she is seen absorbing everyone's souls during the 3I sequence of EoE). They function effectively as recycling or production plants for souls, and it could also be implied they produce LCL as well (Lilith is seen leaking in Terminal Dogma while impaled on the lance). Remember that in NGE, all life is composed to two main components, the soul (usually shown as a little red orb) and LCL. The soul generates the AT field, and in doing so converts the LCL into something more usuable. All in all, the Seeds of Life are necessary for life to form at all. It's fairly easy to conclude that the two types of Seeds weren't meant to land on the same planet, but it could simply be a case of bad luck. First Impact is generally considered synomonous with the Giant Impact Theory , the currently accepted explanation for the formation of the moon (contrary to popular belief, it is not the Chicxulub impact ). Both Seeds arrived around during that point, though it makes little sense if you really look at it. Fortunately, the Giant Impact Theory occurred during one of the "Heavy Bombardment Periods" where everything in the (inner?) solar system was being hit continually by asteroid impacts, so the Seeds arriving need not be exact. It was random chance that both Seeds arrived on Earth. Of course, that doesn't explain why the FAR sent out two incompatible types of Seeds into space. Maybe they were playing the odds and the events of NGE are just bad luck, or just maybe they thought it'd be boring if every just got along. We'll probably never know the answer to that one. The Chamber of Gauf is the source of all souls in Jewish mysticism, and upon being emptied will trigger the end of the world. It could be a referrence to the Seeds, particularly Lilith.... and Rei, who has Lilith's soul. Ritsuko declared "The Chamber of Gauf is empty" as she destroyed all the soulless Rei clones, which is an interesting statement. Whether or not it means Lilith is no longer producing souls is uncertain, but the end of the series (and particularly EoE) are quite literally around the corner at that point. Seems to be a case of mirrorred symbolism, or the mad scientist ramblings of a woman scorned.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Wed Sep 01, 2010 3:57 pm But why not just send the liquid, or the primordial soup? Why even take the risk?

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Wed Sep 01, 2010 4:09 pm BeoX2 wrote: But why not just send the liquid, or the primordial soup? Why even take the risk?

[]: Re: Why would the FAR send a real being instead of just the             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Sep 01, 2010 4:37 pm BeoX2 wrote: We all know that the conflict in NGE is caused by the two seeds of life landing on the same planet. We also know that humanity evolved naturally from the LCL dispersed by Lilith (although we don't know, or at least I don't, how the angels were created from Adam). My question is why not just send the liquid, LCL, out to planets instead of taking the risk of any one being fusing with both beings, like Rei did in EoE? It seems very, well, stupid to take that sort of risk when all you really needed was the LCL. The only reason I can think of is that Lilith was some sort of animal on the FAR's homeworld that naturally made LCL, and so was Adam. But if that's the case, why would the fusing of the two make a godlike creature, if they were simply creatures. Is there any reason why the two Seeds were basically demi-gods, and if so, why would the FAR risk sending them away?

[]: Re: Why would the FAR send a real being instead of just the             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Wed Sep 01, 2010 7:11 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: The FAR had no choice in the matter in sending their people away as their home-world had some bad things going on: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=8703 Lilith and Adam ending up on the same planet was a mistake; even more of a coincidence that 2 of the 7 carriers sent out ended up on the same planet, Earth.

[]: Re: Why would the FAR send a real being instead of just the             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Sep 01, 2010 7:45 pm BeoX2 wrote: So basically, they sent the last of their race out to repopulate other worlds?

[]: Re: Why would the FAR send a real being instead of just the             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Sep 01, 2010 7:56 pm BeoX2 wrote: Jesus, you'd think that that kind of thread would be stickied or something....

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Sep 02, 2010 12:32 pm Angels aren't made of LCL, for starters. Secondly, the Seeds are necessary because without them, there's no one to make the LCL turn from orange juice into living creatures. Same with whatever the PWM is necessary. Your idea is like saying, "Why even bother with a mother? Why not just put an unborn zygote in a room and let it fend for itself?"

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Thu Sep 02, 2010 1:01 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Angels aren't made of LCL, for starters. Secondly, the Seeds are necessary because without them, there's no one to make the LCL turn from orange juice into living creatures. Same with whatever the PWM is necessary. Your idea is like saying, "Why even bother with a mother? Why not just put an unborn zygote in a room and let it fend for itself?"

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Sep 02, 2010 2:52 pm If it was simply LCL that was injected onto the planet, it would have failed in the case of Earth, since it was still molten when the Black Moon crashed near the South Pole and eventually, through plate movement, ended up where Japan is.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Thu Sep 02, 2010 3:13 pm Ornette wrote: If it was simply LCL that was injected onto the planet, it would have failed in the case of Earth, since it was still molten when the Black Moon crashed near the South Pole and eventually, through plate movement, ended up where Japan is.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Sep 02, 2010 4:10 pm BeoX2 wrote: But thinking of that seems to be as big a potential oversight as reckoning that more than one seed would land on the same planet.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Thu Sep 02, 2010 4:57 pm I see your point. But why do these beings have such godlike powers, especially when combined, if they're basically just carriers of the substance that makes life?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Sep 02, 2010 10:43 pm A plastic bubble carrying an embryo is only viable because you have someone watching it to make sure nothing happened. What if some psycho came and popped the bubble with a knife? What if another bubble of LCL came out of nowhere and crashed into it, destroying both of them (the problem with two Seeds on the same planet isn't eliminated if you take out the Seeds and make them just lifeless eggs: The issue was Angels and Lilin on the same planet). As for why they have godlike powers....well, what else but a god can create a new Biosphere out of a dead, molten rock? What else but a god can do something to protect embryonic life if a big ass meteor of death is coming? The Seeds are more than just sperm-dumpsters that poop out a bunch of babies and hope for the best. They need to make sure humans come into existence.

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Thu Sep 09, 2010 9:00 pm My 2 Cents First: let's not affirm for sure that angels aren´t made (at least, partialy) of lcl, in anyway we know that LCL can shape life with a soul in it. So we need more than lcl to seed life in a planet, we need souls, i.e. an energy to drag then to evolve. Second: The SoLs, are (probably) made to give life to XXXX^xx planets in the galaxy, so is not like they can simples AIM AND shoot something without control (A ballon of lcl) As we saw in the EoE, Eva-01 (thus, lillith and others sols) can fly with aritificial At-Field wings, so most likely they are sent to "quadrants" in the galaxy and conscious choose a planet to crash of, after all, if the SoLs were shooted without control, the chance of then missing by microns of orbit calculation could make then fall in jupiter instead of earth, and a a Gas-giant would'tn be able to sustain a sea of lcl based life (as LCL is made of Carbon) Third: a Blob of lcl can´t hold lot's of souls, a SoD carries it's own body wich would give the base material for life to develop but also the energy to it: the Souls, so a special core, (the Gaulf Room) containing the souls must also be transported together with the SoL. Fourth: I think this discussion is beside the point, the reason why the Far and the origin of the Angles weren´t added in the series is to keep it more misterious and cool and to make us focus in the more metaphisical discussion of self-image and psiqué. I think personally the FAR theory is just half baked to make some logical base in the original eva-proposal.
